title: Handshake vs Outreach — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Outreach for LinkedIn automation and sales engagement. Features, pricing, LinkedIn capabilities, and team support compared side-by-side.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a LinkedIn automation platform purpose-built for multi-sender rotation. Teams manage unlimited LinkedIn accounts from a single dashboard with built-in warmup, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies on every plan. Pricing scales by sender count — not per seat — starting at $69/month for 1 sender. Every plan includes unlimited team seats and unlimited workspaces, making it the go-to choice for agencies and sales teams running outreach at scale.
overview.competitor: Outreach is an enterprise sales engagement platform — one of the largest in the category. It's primarily built for email sequences, phone/dialer, and sales workflows. Outreach includes LinkedIn integration as part of its multichannel sequences (primarily manual LinkedIn tasks), CRM sync, AI-powered analytics, pipeline management, and revenue intelligence. Pricing is not publicly listed but typically starts at $100-$150/user/month with annual contracts and minimum seat requirements.

featureComparison.competitor: Enterprise sales engagement across email, phone, and LinkedIn (primarily email-first)

featureComparison.handshake: Full LinkedIn automation — connection requests, messages, follow-ups, profile views, all automated
featureComparison.competitor: LinkedIn integration primarily as manual 'tasks' in sequences — reps are prompted to take LinkedIn actions but most aren't fully automated

featureComparison.handshake: Core feature — automatically rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Not available for LinkedIn — each rep manages their own LinkedIn account within sequences
Industry-leading email sequence engine with smart send times, reply detection, and optimization
Built-in dialer with call recording, transcription, and coaching insights

featureComparison.handshake: Webhook and API integrations for CRM sync
featureComparison.competitor: Deep native CRM integration (Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics) with bidirectional sync, activity logging

featureComparison.handshake: Campaign analytics with A/B testing and performance tracking
featureComparison.competitor: AI-powered revenue intelligence, deal scoring, pipeline analytics, and forecast insights

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox aggregating LinkedIn conversations from all sender accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Unified view across email and LinkedIn tasks per rep — but not aggregated across multiple reps' LinkedIn accounts

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in LinkedIn warmup with gradual ramp-up
featureComparison.competitor: Email warmup available; no LinkedIn-specific warmup

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies, human-like patterns, smart rate limiting for LinkedIn
featureComparison.competitor: Enterprise-grade email deliverability; LinkedIn safety is limited since actions are mostly manual

sections.handshakeWins:
When it comes to LinkedIn specifically, Handshake wins convincingly. Outreach's LinkedIn integration is primarily task-based — it prompts reps to manually visit profiles, send connection requests, and write messages during their sequence. Very little is actually automated on LinkedIn within Outreach.

Handshake fully automates the LinkedIn workflow: connection requests, personalized messages, follow-up sequences, and profile views — all running in the background across multiple sender accounts. The multi-sender rotation alone is a category-defining feature that Outreach doesn't offer.

The cost difference is dramatic. Outreach requires annual contracts, minimum seat counts, and per-user pricing that can easily reach $1,000+/mo for a small team. Handshake's $199/mo for 5 senders with unlimited team seats is a fraction of that cost.

For LinkedIn-first outbound strategies, Handshake delivers more automation, better safety, and vastly better economics.
sections.competitorWins:
Outreach is an enterprise-grade sales engagement platform with capabilities far beyond LinkedIn. Its email sequence engine is industry-leading, with smart send times, reply detection, and sophisticated optimization. The built-in dialer with call recording and coaching is a huge productivity multiplier for phone-heavy teams.

Outreach's CRM integration depth (especially with Salesforce) is unmatched. Activity logging, opportunity syncing, and pipeline analytics all work seamlessly. The AI-powered revenue intelligence features help sales leaders forecast accurately and identify deals at risk.

For enterprise sales organizations that need a comprehensive sales engagement platform — email, phone, LinkedIn tasks, CRM sync, and pipeline analytics — Outreach is the industry standard. It's expensive because it does a lot.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if LinkedIn is your primary or highest-converting outbound channel. It provides full LinkedIn automation, multi-sender rotation, and team pricing that Outreach can't match. Pair it with a separate email tool and CRM for a complete stack at a fraction of Outreach's cost.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Outreach if you're an enterprise sales organization that needs a single platform for email, phone, LinkedIn tasks, CRM integration, and pipeline analytics. It's built for large sales teams with significant budgets who want everything in one place.
sections.verdict: These tools serve different market segments. Outreach is an enterprise sales engagement platform where LinkedIn is one small piece. Handshake is a LinkedIn-first automation platform where LinkedIn is the entire product. If you're choosing based on LinkedIn capabilities alone, Handshake wins hands down — more automation, better safety, multi-sender rotation, and 70-80% lower cost. If you need an all-in-one enterprise platform, Outreach is the incumbent — but you'll pay enterprise prices.

faqs.question: Does Outreach automate LinkedIn actions?
faqs.answer: Partially. Outreach includes LinkedIn as a channel in sequences, but most LinkedIn actions are 'tasks' that prompt reps to take manual action. Connection requests and messages generally need to be sent manually by the rep. Handshake fully automates all LinkedIn actions.

faqs.question: Is Handshake a replacement for Outreach?
faqs.answer: Not directly — they serve different purposes. Handshake replaces the LinkedIn automation component of Outreach (and does it much better). For email and phone, you'd use separate tools. Many teams use Handshake for LinkedIn alongside a separate email tool like Lemlist or Smartlead.

faqs.question: How much does Outreach cost compared to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Outreach typically costs $100-$150 per user per month with annual contracts and minimum seat requirements. Handshake's Growth plan is $199/mo for 5 senders with unlimited team seats — roughly 70-80% less for a team of 5.

faqs.question: Can Handshake integrate with Salesforce like Outreach?
faqs.answer: Handshake offers webhook and API integrations that can sync with Salesforce and other CRMs. It's not as deep as Outreach's native Salesforce integration, but it covers the core use case of syncing LinkedIn activity and lead data to your CRM.

faqs.question: Can I use Handshake and Outreach together?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some teams use Outreach for email and phone sequences while running Handshake for LinkedIn automation. This gives you Outreach's enterprise email capabilities plus Handshake's superior LinkedIn automation — best of both worlds.
